# Community

> *Community nickname: the PISS (Post-Ideological Society System) — because we're done with ideological pissing contests.*

## What This Community Is

A group of people who believe that coordination across genuine difference is both possible and necessary, and who want to contribute to building the intellectual infrastructure for it. We come from different disciplines, different professional contexts, different worldviews and different levels of familiarity with the ideas in this repository. That diversity is a feature, not a problem to be managed.

## What We Value

**Substance over performance.** We care about the quality of thinking, not the credentials behind it. A sharp critique from a nurse who's seen coordination fail on her ward is worth as much as a published paper from a political theorist. Bring what you know.

**Honesty over comfort.** The framework commits to being honest about its own incompleteness. The community must do the same. If something is wrong, say so clearly. If you disagree with a core claim, make the case. Politeness is welcome. Softening a genuine challenge to avoid discomfort is not.

**Engagement over agreement.** You don't need to agree with the framework to contribute. Some of the most valuable contributions will come from people who think the whole approach is mistaken, as long as they can articulate why. Coordination across difference starts here.

**Patience over urgency.** This work is being built carefully. There is no ship date. Contributions that push the thinking forward are always welcome. Contributions that demand immediate action or resolution are less useful. Some tensions need to be held, not solved.

## How to Participate

**Watch and star the repository** if you want to follow development.

**Open an issue** if you have a question, critique, suggestion or case study to share.

**Join a discussion** in the Issues tab — browse existing conversations before starting a new one.

**Contribute content** via pull request or email. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.

**Share the work** if you think someone in your network would find it valuable. The most useful thing a growing community can do is connect the project with people and perspectives it hasn't yet reached.

## Who We're Looking For

The framework claims scale-independence and cross-domain applicability. Testing those claims requires people from:

- **Philosophy and political theory** — to challenge the intellectual foundations
- **Institutional design and public policy** — to test whether the mechanisms could work in practice
- **Defence and security** — to stress-test against high-stakes, high-pressure coordination realities
- **Contemplative and spiritual traditions** — to engage with the experiential dimension
- **Indigenous governance and non-Western coordination traditions** — to broaden the cultural foundations
- **Frontline public servants** (healthcare, education, emergency services) — to ground the theory in operational reality
- **Systems thinking and complexity science** — to refine the theoretical architecture
- **Game theory and mechanism design** — to formalise where formalisation is possible
- **Anyone who's experienced coordination across genuine difference** — at any scale, in any context

## What This Community Is Not

A political movement. A spiritual community. A think tank. A protest. An ideology.

It's a workshop. We're building something. The quality of the build depends on the quality of the people in the room and their willingness to work across the differences between them.
